behaviors.description: Storing a PASS review summary is blocked when any stored dimension finding for the same round has a FAIL verdict.
behaviors.justification: This requires real persistence and gate/write-path wiring between stored per-dimension findings and summary storage validation.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a real test DB with one review round containing at least one dimension verdict=FAIL, then call the real summary-save path with summary verdict PASS for that same round; assert the write is rejected with a validation/gate error and no PASS summary is persisted.

behaviors.description: Stop-gate verdict computation reads per-dimension stored findings directly and does not rely only on hand-written summary text.
behaviors.justification: You must verify computation against conflicting persisted artifacts (stored findings vs summary text), which is a data-wiring/integration concern.
behaviors.test_description: Persist a round where stored findings include FAIL but summary text says all PASS, invoke the real stop-gate verdict computation, and assert the gate verdict is non-PASS based on stored findings.

behaviors.description: Manual summary text overrides require an explicit override flag and produce an auditable warning record.
behaviors.justification: The behavior spans request validation plus durable audit-record creation, which needs real module wiring and storage.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real summary-write endpoint/path twice against real persistence: first with manual override text and no override flag (expect rejection), then with override flag enabled (expect success) and assert an audit warning record is written with actor, round, and override reason metadata.

behaviors.description: After Phase 4 fixes, the gate requires a review round identifier produced after the latest fix commit before PASS can be finalized.
behaviors.justification: To catch real regressions, this needs real commit identity/timestamp ordering behavior and gating logic across commit history and round metadata.
behaviors.test_description: Create two real commits in a temp repo/subprocess flow, run/create a review round tied to the older commit, apply a fix commit, then attempt PASS finalization using the stale round id; assert finalization is blocked until a new post-fix round id exists.

behaviors.description: In the live review loop, PASS cannot finalize unless a fresh review-agent round runs on the updated diff.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end sequencing guarantee across fix commit, diff update, agent round execution, and finalization decision.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the live pipeline sequence: start review, apply code changes, attempt PASS without launching a new review-agent round (expect block), then run a new round on the updated diff and retry finalization; assert PASS is only possible after the fresh round step completes.

behaviors.description: If review rounds are exhausted with unresolved MUST-FIX findings in stored results, the outcome is escalation rather than PASS.
behaviors.justification: This can be validated by persisted round state, finding severities, and outcome computation without requiring live agent execution.
behaviors.test_description: Seed persisted review state with max rounds reached and at least one unresolved MUST-FIX finding, invoke the real outcome computation/finalization path, and assert outcome=ESCALATION (and explicitly not PASS).

behaviors.description: In a live multi-round review workflow, exhausted rounds with unresolved MUST-FIX findings still block PASS and emit escalation.
behaviors.justification: This requires exercising the full multi-round loop behavior over time, including repeated rounds, unresolved findings carry-over, exhaustion, and escalation emission.
behaviors.test_description: Run an end-to-end multi-round review workflow where MUST-FIX findings persist across rounds until the configured round limit is hit; assert PASS finalization remains blocked and an escalation event/record is emitted at exhaustion.
